channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Republican congressman suggests students get jobs to pay for lunches
item.description: Republican Rep. Ritch McCormick (R-Ga.) suggested that students should get jobs to pay for their lunches as the federal funding freeze proposed by President Trump threatens nutrition support for children across the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Trump approval rating slips after flurry of executive actions: Survey
item.description: President Trump's approval rating slipped after his first week back in the Oval Office, where he issued a slew of executive orders.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Live updates: Democrats press RFK Jr. on past vaccine positions at fiery hearing
item.description: President Trump's executive actions, including a freeze on federal grants and a buyout offer to federal employees, have drawn criticism from Democrats and some Republicans, while his pick for HHS Secretary, Robert F. Kennedy, Jr.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, ABC 4

item.title: Frontier makes a second bid for Spirit with budget airlines struggling
item.description: Frontier Airlines is attempting for a second time to merge with the now bankrupt Spirit Airlines, which declared bankruptcy late last year as budget airlines struggle.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump makes moves to expand power, sparking chaos, possible constitutional crisis
item.description: Just a little over a week into his second term, President Donald Trump is taking steps to maximize his power, sparking chaos and what critics contend is a constitutional crisis as he challenges the separation of powers that have defined American government for more than 200 years.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Defense secretary pulls Trump critic Gen. Milley's security clearance and protective detail
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is pulling the security protections and clearance of retired Joint Chiefs chairman Gen. Mark Milley.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Top NYPD officials faulted for 'demeaning,' 'unprofessional' social media posts attacking critics
item.description: Two NYPD chiefs violated department policy when they made "demeaning" social media posts to attack civil servants and journalists, an independent monitor found.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A $4B settlement for Hawaii wildfire victims is in legal limbo as an unusual trial starts
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — When Hawaii Gov. Josh Green announced a $4 billion settlement about a year after the deadliest U.S. wildfire in a century devastated Lahaina in 2023, he touted the speed of the deal to “avoid protracted and painful lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge set to sentence former Sen. Bob Menendez, who was convicted of taking bribes of cash and gold
item.description: Former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez faces the possibility of a long prison term when he is sentenced Wednesday for taking bribes of gold bars, a luxury car and hundreds of thousands of dollars in cash.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Flawed emergency alert systems lagged when residents needed them most during Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: When disaster strikes, government emergency alert systems offer a simple promise: Residents will get information about nearby dangers and instructions to help them stay safe.
item.dc:creator: Christopher L. Keller, Claudia Lauer, Amy Taxin And Rebecca Boone, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Alyssa Thomas heading to Mercury from Sun in trade, AP source says
item.description: The Phoenix Mercury were finalizing a deal to acquire Alyssa Thomas from the Connecticut Sun, a person familiar with the trade told The Associated Press on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A$AP Rocky's accuser says he was stunned and furious when his old friend pulled a gun on him
item.description: A$AP Rocky's accuser, former friend and the key witness at his trial testified Tuesday that their relationship had been fraying for years, but he was “furious” and flabbergasted when Rocky pulled a gun on him on the streets of Hollywood.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sisters convicted in planning murder-for-hire scheme
item.description: FAIRHOPE, Ala. (WKRG) — Two Fairhope sisters were convicted on multiple charges in a murder-for-hire scheme, according to the United States Department of Justice.
item.dc:creator: Pat O'Donnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Carnivore diet sends man to hospital with hands filled with cholesterol
item.description: The patient was eventually diagnosed with a condition known as xanthelasma — the build-up of cholesterol under the skin.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Florida congresswoman wants Trump's face to be carved on Mount Rushmore
item.description: The Mount Rushmore National Memorial in South Dakota currently features the 60-foot-tall heads of four U.S. presidents.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, NewsNation

item.title: Man busted with 15 pounds of meth, 13 pounds of cocaine in Florida: Deputies
item.description: A search warrant on his Volusia County home revealed cash and packaging consistent with narcotics trafficking.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: New Homeland Security secretary joins federal agents to tout immigrant arrests in NYC
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem joined federal agents Tuesday in New York City to announce the arrests of immigrants wanted on criminal charges, including a man accused of kidnapping who was seen on a viral video entering a Colorado apartment with reputed gang members.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disturbing messages seen in empty lot in downtown Los Angeles are not nefarious: LAPD
item.description: A patch of undeveloped land in downtown Los Angeles is going viral on social media for some distressing messages seen from above, but police have confirmed that they found no illicit activity in the area.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC home to hundreds of thousands of undocumented people: Reports
item.description: The Trump administration is pushing to increase the arrests of undocumented people, issuing daily quotas for the tri-state area, according to The Washington Post.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration to offer buyouts to all federal workers ahead of return to office
item.description: The Trump administration is offering all 2 million federal employees what amounts to a buyout if they do not intend to return to work in person later this year, sources confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in US history hits Kansas, officials say
item.description: The Kansas Department of Health and Environment (KDHE) says Kansas is facing the largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in American history.
item.dc:creator: Colter Robinson, KRON4

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump admin's freeze on federal grants, loans
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked a Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans that could total trillions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge has temporarily blocked a Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans that could total trillions of dollars. U.S. District Judge Loren L. AliKhan blocked the action Tuesday afternoon, minutes before it was set to go into effect.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A GOP lawsuit to limit people in the US illegally from the census count will be harmful, voters say
item.description: Voters in California and Texas affiliated with a Democratic group want to intervene in a lawsuit filed by four Republican state attorneys general that is attempting to exclude people in the U.S. illegally from numbers used to divvy up congressional seats among states.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right recommendations on health issues
item.description: Fewer than half of Americans in a new poll say they trust President Trump or his health-related nominees to make recommendations on the nation's pressing health care issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson brushes aside economic concerns associated with ICE raids
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is brushing aside economic concerns associated with the raids that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officials have carried out in recent days, as Democrats warn that the mass deportations could harm the economy.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Freighter on the move after it was freed from ice on frozen Lake Erie
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — A freighter was on its way to Canada on Sunday after it was freed from ice that trapped it in a frozen Lake Erie for days, the U.S. Coast Guard said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More DEI fallout: Air Force scraps course that used videos of Tuskegee Airmen and female WWII pilots
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Air Force has removed training courses with videos of its storied Tuskegee Airmen and the Women Airforce Service Pilots, or WASPs — the female World War II pilots who were vital in ferrying warplanes for the military — to comply with the Trump administration's crackdown on dive
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What is a migrant? What is ICE? 10 terms to help you understand the debate over immigration
item.description: Understanding the many different immigration terms – some technical, some not – can help people better understand immigration news.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Castañeda Professor of Latin American and Latino studies, American University, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's whirlwind week back at the White House
item.description: President Trump stormed into the White House with a whirlwind of a first week, quickly making good on a number of campaign promises while embracing the trappings of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: At Paris Fashion Week, Hermes menswear has a dash of jockey-style boldness
item.description: Hermes illuminated a gloomy Paris afternoon with its fall men’s collection Saturday, as Veronique Nichanian turned to the racetrack for inspiration.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Private investigator first to admit guilt in offering bribes to get intoxicated drivers off the hook
item.description: A private investigator embroiled in a corruption scandal that rocked New Mexico’s law enforcement community for over a decade was the first to plead guilty to a slew of federal charges that include racketeering and bribery, according to court records released Friday.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oklahoma bill would make it a crime punishable by jail time to "possess" a shopping cart belonging to a business
item.description: An Oklahoma lawmaker is proposing a law that would make it a crime punishable by up to a year in jail for anyone caught “in possession of” a shopping cart taken from a store.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, ABC 4
